title: ptychopyxis kingii
intro: Ptychopyxis kingii, also known as King's Pilea, is a species of flowering plant in the family Phyllanthaceae. It has been traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which have been attributed to the presence of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ptychopyxis kingii include its potential to treat fever, sore throats, and skin conditions, as well as its anti-diarrheal and anti-ulcer properties. Herbal preparations of Ptychopyxis kingii can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is often used in traditional medicine in the form of a decoction or a poultice.
